message:
chore(beads): close frankenredis-s5su (HIGH: RDB stream encoding parent)

All 5 sub-beads shipped — k2lp (docs), 3g0p (listpack primitive),
hjub (type-15 entry decoder), qi6z (consumer-group reification for
types 19/21), 6zk9 (upstream encoder + vendored XINFO STREAM FULL
round-trip across 20 fixtures). Cross-cutting 1M-iteration differential
fuzz gate remains tracked as frankenredis-1749.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -577,7 +577,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-s26k","title":"[MOCK] XREAD BLOCK is still a documented non-blocking stub","description":"Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:19814 documents "Integer BLOCK values are accepted (non-blocking stub)" in the XREAD regression.

Current gap: XREAD parses BLOCK but does not yet drive real blocked-client waiting/wakeup behavior, so callers only exercise a placeholder non-blocking path.

Real impl path: complete BLOCK handling in the XREAD command path in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs and hook it into blocked-client scheduling / stream wakeups in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:43:51.161562995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:33:13.846327788Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:33:13.846327788Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-s4p","title":"Wire BLMPOP and BZMPOP to server blocking infrastructure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:41:47.618358935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T03:45:05.128321907Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T03:45:05.127728385Z","close_reason":"Added BLmpop/BZmpop to BlockingOp, detection in try_build_blocked_state (timeout from argv[1]), fulfillment via re-execution in try_fulfill_blocked, nil timeout response","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-s5nk","title":"Add metamorphic property tests for HyperLogLog (PFADD, PFCOUNT, PFMERGE)","description":"Add comprehensive metamorphic tests for HyperLogLog: Idempotency, Order-Invariance, Merge Commutativity, Merge Associativity, and Subset Relation using proptest in fr-store.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:27:44.689027431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:29:11.851554666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:29:11.849743660Z","close_reason":"HLL metamorphic tests already exist in metamorphic.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-s5su","title":"HIGH: RDB type 15 (stream) encoding diverges from upstream — file interchange broken","description":"## Summary

Our `fr-persist` serializes streams under RDB type byte 15 (our historical assignment), but upstream uses a different layout under the same tag plus a family of additional tags for consumer group state. Project memory note `project_rdb_stream_incompatibility.md`: \"RDB type 15 stream encoding differs from Redis, breaking file interchange\".

Impact: any user who takes an upstream `dump.rdb` and boots our server on it gets a parse failure or (worse) silently-truncated stream state. Symmetrically, our RDB cannot be loaded by upstream. That violates AGENTS.md Compatibility Doctrine strict mode, which promises \"maximize observable compatibility for V1-scoped APIs\" with \"no behavior-altering repairs\".

## Surface to verify

Upstream `rdb.h` / `rdb.c` (see `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.h` and `rdb.c`):

- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS` (15) — listpack-bodied stream
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_2` (19) — adds per-consumer seen_time
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` (21) — adds per-consumer active_time (7.2+)

Each shape writes:
1. radix tree: (stream-id, listpack)
2. stream metadata: length, last_id, first_id, max_deleted_id, entries_added
3. per consumer group: name, last_id, PEL size, PEL entries, consumer list
4. per consumer: name, seen_time (v2+), active_time (v3+), pending entry IDs

Our encoder should write v3 by default (matches upstream 7.2+) and decode all three.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-fuzzing

**Conformance (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip):**
- Fixture corpus: 20 upstream-produced `.rdb` files with stream shapes covering each axis (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time).
- Round-trip gate:
  1. `fr-persist::rdb::decode(bytes)` must succeed on every fixture.
  2. Re-encode; feed to upstream `redis-server --loadrdb`, query `XINFO STREAM FULL`; canonicalize and compare to our canonicalized `XINFO STREAM FULL` post-load.
  3. Reverse: encode our in-memory stream, have upstream load it, compare stream state vector.

**Fuzzing (/testing-fuzzing, structure-aware):**
- Extend the existing `fuzz_rdb_structured.rs` with a stream-producing `arbitrary::Arbitrary` generator: random entries, groups, PELs. Invariant: for any generated shape, `decode(encode(x)) == x` AND upstream can load the encoded bytes (this last check runs gated, driven by a `REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1` env var).
- Extend `fuzz_rdb_decoder.rs` with upstream-produced corpus (minimized from the 20 fixtures above) so crashes on adversarial streams are found quickly.

## Related

- Frames our broader persistence story for streams; the `core_stream.json` conformance fixtures already exist (`crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_stream.json`, 114 KB).
- Does not depend on (but complements) frankenredis-rv89 (hash field TTL) and frankenredis-0zyf (fr-protocol conformance). All three share the upstream-fixture harvesting infra.

## Acceptance

- 20/20 upstream fixtures decode cleanly.
- Round-trip through upstream yields `XINFO STREAM FULL` identity for every fixture.
- Fuzz target logs zero crashes / zero mismatches over 1M iterations.

## Labels

fr-persist, rdb, streams, conformance-gap, file-interchange, testing-conformance-harnesses","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T03:30:14.504545661Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:13:53.323992378Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance-gap","file-interchange","fr-persist","rdb","streams","testing-conformance-harnesses"]}

Acceptance gate 3 (1M differential fuzz) is tracked as frankenredis-1749 — the shared cross-cutting fuzz gate for v8s5 + s5su."}
 {"id":"frankenredis-s6la","title":"Tighten integer parsing for ACL LOG/SELECT/SCAN COUNT + set encoding","description":"Fixes non-canonical integer acceptance: ACL LOG count, SELECT DB index, SCAN COUNT in runtime and HSCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN count parsing. Also ensure OBJECT ENCODING set uses strict integer detection so '01' is not treated as intset.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:44:06.367735867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:46:58.908198817Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:46:58.907869644Z","close_reason":"Strict integer parsing for ACL LOG/SELECT/SCAN COUNT + set encoding + fixture update","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-s6op","title":"Add fuzz target for an uncovered Redis parser/state machine","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T11:14:42.143025248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T11:25:29.906597673Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T11:25:29.904770924Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-sa3w","title":"Activate FR-P2C-001 eventloop journey in the live-oracle matrix","description":"Evidence: crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/user_workflow_corpus_v1.json still marks the FR-P2C-001 differential_hook as planned even though crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/fr_p2c_001_eventloop_journey.json already exists and contains only deterministic PING/SET/GET/unknown-command/wrong-arity cases that are suitable for upstream Redis differential runs. The fixture is still absent from crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json.\n\nNeed: promote FR-P2C-001 from planned to active by wiring the eventloop journey fixture into the live-oracle matrix and adding the corresponding live-oracle test/orchestrator coverage so the user-workflow corpus and upstream differential harness stop drifting apart.\n\nReal impl path: crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/user_workflow_corpus_v1.json, crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json, crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/fr_p2c_001_eventloop_journey.json, and crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs live-oracle coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:20:31.946235324Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:44:30.710376562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:44:30.708089263Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","eventloop","fr-conformance","testing"]}
